request.System: Note: if a tool_result begins with `[CANCELLED]`, the tool call was interrupted by the user. Do not retry it automatically; acknowledge the interruption and ask the user how to proceed.

Content.Text: Create a task list: 1) Read hello.txt, 2) Count the words, 3) Write the count to count.txt. Then execute each task in order, updating the task list as you go.

Tools.Name: Bash
Tools.Description: Execute a shell command via bash -c. Cancellation kills the whole process group.

Tools.Name: Edit
Tools.Description: Replace an exact string in a file inside the workdir. Use replace_all to handle multiple occurrences.

Tools.Name: Grep
Tools.Description: Recursively search workdir for a Go regex; emits path:line:content for each match. Honors .gitignore and built-in default excludes (node_modules, dist, etc.) by default.

ignore_vcs.description: respect .gitignore (default: follow config; usually true). Set false to grep into ignored paths.

hidden.description: walk into dot-files/dirs (default false). .git/.hg/.svn are still always skipped.

Tools.Name: Read
Tools.Description: Read the contents of a file in the session workdir. Output is line-numbered.

path.description: file path, relative to workdir or absolute

Tools.Name: TodoWrite
Tools.Description: Maintain a structured task list for the current session so the user can see overall progress. Pass the COMPLETE list every call — it replaces the previous list (omitting a task drops it). Use it for non-trivial work of three or more steps. Before starting a step set its status to 'in_progress'; the moment it is done set it to 'completed' — update in real time, don't batch. Keep exactly one task in_progress at a time. Skip the list for single, trivial actions and just do them. Each task: 'subject' (short imperative title), 'status' (pending|in_progress|completed, default pending), optional 'description' and 'active_form' (present-tense label shown while in progress).

tasks.description: The complete task list. Replaces the previous list in full.

subject.description: Short imperative title, e.g. "Add retry to client".

description.description: Optional detail of what the task entails.

active_form.description: Optional present-tense label shown while the task is in progress, e.g. "Adding retry".

Tools.Name: Write
Tools.Description: Atomically replace a file inside the session workdir.

Tools.Name: memory_read
Tools.Description: Read the current content of a memory file from disk.

kind.description: Which memory file to read: 'memory' for agent-curated facts, 'user' for user identity and preferences.

Tools.Name: memory_write
Tools.Description: Write content to a memory file. Use mode 'replace' (default) or 'append'.

mode.description: Write mode: 'replace' (default) overwrites the file; 'append' adds after existing content.

Tools.Name: session_search
Tools.Description: Search historical messages across sessions using full-text search.

query.description: The search query. Supports ASCII words and CJK characters.

session_id.description: The current session ID, used for scope resolution.

scope.description: Search scope: 'session' for current session only, 'all' for all non-deleted sessions. Default is the session tree (current + ancestors + descendants).

context_before.description: Number of messages before each hit for context (default 5, max 20).

context_after.description: Number of messages after each hit for context (default 5, max 20).

Tools.Description: Recursively search workdir for a Go regex; emits path:line:content for each match. Honors .gitignore and built-in default excludes (node_modules, dist, etc.) by default.

ignore_vcs.description: respect .gitignore (default: follow config; usually true). Set false to grep into ignored paths.

hidden.description: walk into dot-files/dirs (default false). .git/.hg/.svn are still always skipped.

Tools.Name: Read
Tools.Description: Read the contents of a file in the session workdir. Output is line-numbered.

path.description: file path, relative to workdir or absolute

Tools.Name: TodoWrite
Tools.Description: Maintain a structured task list for the current session so the user can see overall progress. Pass the COMPLETE list every call — it replaces the previous list (omitting a task drops it). Use it for non-trivial work of three or more steps. Before starting a step set its status to 'in_progress'; the moment it is done set it to 'completed' — update in real time, don't batch. Keep exactly one task in_progress at a time. Skip the list for single, trivial actions and just do them. Each task: 'subject' (short imperative title), 'status' (pending|in_progress|completed, default pending), optional 'description' and 'active_form' (present-tense label shown while in progress).

tasks.description: The complete task list. Replaces the previous list in full.

subject.description: Short imperative title, e.g. "Add retry to client".

description.description: Optional detail of what the task entails.

active_form.description: Optional present-tense label shown while the task is in progress, e.g. "Adding retry".

Tools.Name: Write
Tools.Description: Atomically replace a file inside the session workdir.

Tools.Name: memory_read
Tools.Description: Read the current content of a memory file from disk.

kind.description: Which memory file to read: 'memory' for agent-curated facts, 'user' for user identity and preferences.

Tools.Name: memory_write
Tools.Description: Write content to a memory file. Use mode 'replace' (default) or 'append'.

mode.description: Write mode: 'replace' (default) overwrites the file; 'append' adds after existing content.

Tools.Name: session_search
Tools.Description: Search historical messages across sessions using full-text search.

query.description: The search query. Supports ASCII words and CJK characters.

session_id.description: The current session ID, used for scope resolution.

scope.description: Search scope: 'session' for current session only, 'all' for all non-deleted sessions. Default is the session tree (current + ancestors + descendants).

context_before.description: Number of messages before each hit for context (default 5, max 20).

context_after.description: Number of messages after each hit for context (default 5, max 20).

request.System: Note: if a tool_result begins with `[CANCELLED]`, the tool call was interrupted by the user. Do not retry it automatically; acknowledge the interruption and ask the user how to proceed.

Total: 15 words.
